faqs.question: Why are silverfish so common in Tremont buildings?
faqs.answer: Tremont's many pre-war buildings often have higher humidity levels due to older plumbing, basement moisture, and limited ventilation—conditions silverfish love. These pests feed on starches, paper, and fabrics, making historic Bronx apartments with original features particularly vulnerable. Our treatments address both the pests and moisture sources.
